title: AMC Entertainment Holdings Inc Reports Q2 2024 Earnings
text:
AMC Q2 2024 Earnings Summary and Analysis

AMC reported total revenue of $1.03 billion and a loss of $32.8 million in the second quarter of 2024. Total revenues were $1,030.6 million, a 23.5% decrease from $1,347.9 million in Q2 2023. Despite the decline, the quarter marked an improvement from Q1 2024 performance.

Attendance Metrics: Attendance was 50.0 million, a decline of 24.6% year-over-year. More specifically, U.S. markets saw a 27.0% drop in attendance, while international markets experienced a 17.3% decline. The attendance decline was primarily attributed to a weaker film slate compared to 2023.

Revenue Breakdown: Q2 2024 admissions revenue fell 24.1% year-over-year to $564.4 million; food and beverage revenue dropped 24.8% to $367.1 million. Adjusted EBITDA saw a significant decline to $29.4 million from $182.5 million.

Positive Signal: The quarter ended on a strong note, with AMC recording the highest-ever opening weekend attendance and highest-ever opening weekend admissions revenue for a rated-R movie following the success of Deadpool & Wolverine. This success provided momentum heading into Q3 2024.

Context: The Q2 2024 results reflected broader industry challenges from fewer blockbusters released during the quarter, though management emphasized the upcoming strong slate of releases for the remainder of 2024.
